key_insight: The paper derives and numerically validates shape derivatives for thermoelastic topology optimization when material properties depend on temperature, enabling coupled thermal-structural design updates.
engineering_relevance: This matters for designing lightweight structures that must remain mechanically safe under thermal loading, such as components in energy, aerospace, and high-temperature machinery.
method: Prove existence of the shape derivative for thermoelastic problems with temperature-dependent parameters
Derive a generic domain-integral expression for shape gradients
Solve state equations with finite elements and evolve the geometry with a level set method
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical results for two benchmark optimization setups: compliance minimization with a volume constraint and volume minimization with a von Mises stress constraint.
watch_item: Implementation can be sensitive to the accuracy of coupled thermal-mechanical solves and the stability of the level-set update when material parameters vary strongly with temperature.
paper_title: Shape optimization for thermoelasticity with temperature-dependent material parameters
